forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KW2688BRDE3F4A8ATA43F48R
+- 01KW26600YD3PM22S8F4VJJTE5
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: b880
+project: expect
+title: Tier 3 model judgment (gated, residual-of-residual) + escalation queue
+---
+## What
+Add the top rung: rubric model judgment against the withheld criteria, only on the residual the cheap tiers couldn't decide. Per `ideas/expect.md` §"The Verdict Ladder" Tier 3, §"Tier 3 is the residual-of-the-residual", and §"Reliability and Non-Determinism" (grading hardening).
+
+- In `crates/swissarmyhammer-expect/src/evaluate.rs` (+ grader module):
+  - A judgment criterion compiles to `{rubric, anchor: <approved text>, sim_threshold}`. At evaluate: locate evidence → first take **embedding similarity to the anchor**; ≥ threshold ⇒ pass with NO model call. Only on divergence does the judge wake: "does this NEW evidence still satisfy the rubric?" Yes ⇒ passes rubric but evidence changed ⇒ DRIFT (re-approval). No ⇒ fail.
+  - The grader is a named sah `model:` (resolved like review/rules; pinned in the golden), DISTINCT from the driving agent. Grade is binary form-filling per criterion (one observable criterion at a time), chain-of-thought + evidence, NOT free-form scoring. Use the ACP grading path (model call; if it must act, an independent ACP subagent) — but the grader must NOT be the same model that drove the run.
+  - Optional small **panel** of named models for borderline criteria (config `[model].panel`); disagreement is itself a signal of a vague criterion.
+  - **Escalation queue**: criteria resolved with low confidence (below `[approval].escalate_below_confidence`, per-surface tunable) are routed to a human queue, not auto-passed.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] A judgment criterion whose evidence matches the anchor (sim ≥ threshold) passes WITHOUT a model call (assert no model invocation).
+- [ ] Divergent-but-rubric-satisfying evidence ⇒ drift; rubric-failing ⇒ fail.
+- [ ] Tier 3 runs only on the residual after Tiers 1-2; the grading model is pinned and is not the driver.
+- [ ] Low-confidence criteria land in the escalation queue rather than auto-passing; an optional panel records disagreement.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Evaluate tests with a stub grader + stub embedder: anchor-match short-circuit (no grader called), divergence→drift, fail case, escalation-on-low-confidence.
+- [ ] Panel test: disagreement flagged.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-expect tier3` passes.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd`. Stub the grader/embedder for determinism.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -6,6 +6,13 @@
   id: 01kw3z9pmvmnt0j0r7227trssk
   text: 'Picked up. Research done: evaluate.rs has Tier 1 (evaluate/evaluate_assertion) and Tier 2 (ToleranceAssertion/ToleranceBand/evaluate_tiered + TextEmbedder seam with StubEmbedder/PanicEmbedder test patterns). config.rs has ModelConfig.panel + ApprovalConfig.escalate_below_confidence (default 0.6). ledger.rs has GradingPins + stub tolerance_drift/judgment_drift. Plan: new grader.rs module with Grader trait (binary form-filling), JudgmentAssertion {checkpoint,locator,anchor,sim_threshold,rubric}, residual-of-residual resolve (embed anchor sim >= threshold => pass NO grader call; divergence => grade => pass-rubric=DRIFT / fail=FAIL), optional panel (disagreement flagged), driver-distinct check; extend evaluate_tiered to add Tier3 + return TieredVerdict{verdict, escalations}. TDD with stub grader + stub embedder.'
   timestamp: 2026-06-27T07:21:55.099378+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kw3znhg22h1y6t91yz530n6b
+  text: |-
+    Implemented Tier 3 in new crates/swissarmyhammer-expect/src/grader.rs (Grader trait + Grade/GradeRequest binary form-filling; JudgmentAssertion residual-of-residual resolve: embed anchor sim >= threshold => pass with NO grader call; divergence => grade => rubric-pass=JUDGMENT_DRIFT_REASON drift / rubric-fail=fail; panel disagreement => PANEL_DISAGREEMENT_REASON + zero confidence; grader==driver => GRADER_IS_DRIVER_REASON escalation). Extended evaluate_tiered to run Tier3 only on the residual after 1-2 and return TieredVerdict{verdict, escalations}; escalation_for routes confidence < escalate_below_confidence to the queue (never auto-passed). cosine_similarity made pub(crate) and reused. 7 grader tier3 tests + 2 new evaluate_tiered tests (residual ordering, escalation). Full crate green 183/183; clippy -D warnings clean; cargo check --workspace ok; fmt applied.
+
+    DECISION on ledger.rs tolerance/judgment compare stubs: left as-is. The card says replace "if straightforward" — it is NOT. compare()/compare_criterion re-derive verdicts via evaluate(obs, golden.assertions) which only carries Tier 1 CompiledAssertion (the golden does not yet store ToleranceAssertion/JudgmentAssertion), and a real tolerance/judgment compare needs an embedder + bands threaded through the whole ledger API (compare/ledger_state/ledger_entry/ledger_queue/approval_*). That is significant blast radius and depends on the golden storing Tier 2/3 assertions first. Tracked as a follow-up task.
+  timestamp: 2026-06-27T07:28:23.042928+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KW2688BRDE3F4A8ATA43F48R
 - 01KW26600YD3PM22S8F4VJJTE5
